Title: NUCLEOCYTOPLASMIC shuttling of ETHYLENE RESPONSE FACTOR 5 mediated by nitric oxide suppresses ethylene biosynthesis in apple fruit

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced by GSNO treatment; increased by cPTIO treatment; increased when MdERF5 is silenced
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced by GSNO treatment; increased by cPTIO treatment; increased when MdERF5 is silenced
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its transcription is directly repressed by MdERF5, contributing to lowered ethylene production under NO.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; GUS transactivation; MdERF5 silencing in calli and fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACS1 expression is suppressed by NO donor treatment and promoted by NO scavenging. 2. MdERF5 directly binds the DRE motif in the MdACS1 promoter and represses its transcription. 3. MdERF5 silencing increases MdACS1 expression and ethylene production under GSNO treatment.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GSNO/SNP treatment; cPTIO treatment; postharvest storage
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as the central rate-limiting enzyme in ethylene biosynthesis, but the paper experimentally focuses on its transcriptional repression by MdERF5 rather than ACS1 catalytic characterization.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter editing or regulator-guided suppression of MdACS1 could be used to reduce ethylene and extend shelf life, but excessive repression may impair normal ripening.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ACC to ethylene in the final step of ethylene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in apple fruit during ripening; transcript reduced by GSNO treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in apple fruit during ripening; transcript reduced by GSNO treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at room temperature; GSNO or SNP NO-donor treatment; cPTIO NO-scavenger treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its enzyme activity is suppressed by NO-activated MdERF5, reducing ethylene production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; Y2H; pull-down; co-IP; BiFC; luciferase complementation; in vitro enzyme activity assay; fruit ACO activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACO1 is a key ethylene biosynthetic enzyme catalyzing ACC to ethylene. 2. MdERF5 directly interacts with MdACO1, and GSNO enhances this interaction. 3. Increasing MdERF5 protein suppresses MdACO1 enzymatic activity in vitro and GSNO suppresses ACO activity in fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdERF5 interacts with the MdACO1D fragment containing the putative Fe(II)-binding site, suggesting inhibition may involve interference with Fe(II) cofactor binding. MdACO1 transcription is reduced by GSNO, but MdERF5 silencing did not significantly alter MdACO1 transcript in calli, implying additional regulators act on MdACO1 transcription.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of MdACO1 would delay ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to delay climacteric ripening and extend shelf life, but excessive suppression may impair normal ripening and quality development.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family; ERF domain; contains predicted NLS region with Ser260
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of MdACS1 and protein interaction-mediated inhibition of MdACO1 activity; nucleocytoplasmic shuttling controlled by dephosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses MdACS1 transcription; suppresses MdACO1 enzyme activity through physical interaction
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitric oxide; GSNO/SNP treatment; endogenous NO decline during fruit development; MdPP2C57-mediated dephosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated by GSNO during postharvest storage; decreases during fruit development; reduced by cPTIO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated by GSNO during postharvest storage; decreases during fruit development; reduced by cPTIO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; shuttles from nucleus to cytoplasm after NO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms interaction complexes with MdPP2C57 in the nucleus and with MdACO1 in nucleus/cytoplasm depending on NO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator-independent suppressor required for NO-mediated repression of ethylene biosynthesis during apple fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NO-mediated suppression of ethylene production by repressing MdACS1 and inhibiting MdACO1 activity; silencing MdERF5 increases ethylene production under GSNO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; RNAi/transient antisense silencing in calli and fruit; immunoblot; subcellular localization; phospho-mutant analysis; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-PCR; GUS transactivation; Y2H; pull-down; co-IP; BiFC; luciferase complementation; ethylene and ACO activity assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdERF5 is induced by NO and is required for NO-suppressed ethylene biosynthesis in apple fruit. 2. In the nucleus, MdERF5 binds the DRE motif in the MdACS1 promoter and represses MdACS1 transcription. 3. In the cytoplasm, MdERF5 interacts with MdACO1 and suppresses its enzyme activity. 4. NO promotes MdERF5 nucleocytoplasmic shuttling through MdPP2C57-dependent dephosphorylation at Ser260.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest storage at room temperature; GSNO or SNP NO-donor treatment; cPTIO treatment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes faster ripening and higher ethylene; overactivation would likely delay ripening and may alter normal fruit softening and flavor development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target for extending apple shelf life by reducing ethylene biosynthesis; engineering should balance delayed ripening with acceptable fruit quality.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phosphorylated MdERF5 shows stronger repression of MdACS1 promoter activity, whereas dephosphorylation at Ser260 promotes cytoplasmic relocalization. Thus NO increases both nuclear and cytoplasmic MdERF5 pools, enabling dual suppression of ethylene biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-translational regulation of ethylene biosynthesis via regulator shuttling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: dephosphorylation of MdERF5 at Ser260, promoting nuclear export/nucleocytoplasmic shuttling and enhancing MdERF5 repression of ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly strengthens MdERF5-mediated repression of MdACS1; indirectly enhances suppression of MdACO1 activity by promoting MdERF5 relocalization
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitric oxide; GSNO treatment activates MdPP2C57 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated by GSNO in fruit; decreases during fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated by GSNO in fruit; decreases during fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with MdERF5 in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller that links NO signaling to MdERF5 relocalization and suppression of ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dephosphorylates MdERF5 and promotes its shuttling from nucleus to cytoplasm, thereby strengthening NO-mediated suppression of ethylene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; inhibitor assay with sanguinarine chloride; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; luciferase complementation; in vitro and in vivo phosphatase assays; co-expression localization assay; EMSA; GUS transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPP2C57 is a NO-induced PP2C phosphatase associated with ethylene suppression. 2. It interacts with MdERF5 in the nucleus and dephosphorylates MdERF5. 3. Dephosphorylation at Ser260 is required for MdERF5 nucleocytoplasmic shuttling. 4. MdPP2C57 enhances MdERF5 binding/repression of MdACS1 and promotes MdERF5-mediated inhibition of ethylene biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GSNO treatment; PP2C inhibitor sanguinarine chloride; postharvest storage
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced PP2C57 activity may delay ripening via ethylene suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for postharvest ripening control through modulation of MdERF5 localization and activity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GSNO enhances MdPP2C57-MdERF5 interaction. MdPP2C57 localization itself is not altered by GSNO. The phosphomimetic MdERF5S260D is not exported by MdPP2C57, supporting Ser260 as the critical site.
